name: brokenlink_legacy_replacement_url_worker_noop
notes: Documents a real Worker gap (not a normal case): applyBrokenLinkFixes in seojuice-ssr.js only reads fix.new_url ('const newUrl = fix.new_url || "";') and never falls back to fix.replacement_url. When new_url is empty (the legacy field-change shape) the fix is silently skipped ('action === replace && !newUrl -> continue') and the broken link is left untouched. expected_html therefore equals input_html unchanged. Per the umbrella plan (spec delta, GENERAL plan Global Constraints (a)), each SDK is expected to read `new_url || replacement_url` — that fallback is a deliberate SDK-side delta from the raw Worker and is verified by each SDK's own unit tests (see node sub-plan Task 6), NOT by this shared vector. This vector exists to pin the Worker's current (gap) behavior so nobody accidentally 'fixes' an SDK back to Worker-for-Worker parity here.
